description: English organized crime group based in Bethnal Green, London, led by the Kray twins from the late 1950s until their arrest in 1968. Involved in murder, armed robbery, arson, protection rackets, gambling, and assaults. The twins were convicted in 1969 and sentenced to life imprisonment.

country_links.evidence_quote: The following year, a Montreal branch of the Royal Bank of Canada was robbed of C$ 50,000 in bearer bonds. ... The Montreal-based Cotroni crime family ... decided to sell the stolen bonds in Britain through the Krays.
country_links.context: The Firm received stolen bearer bonds from a Montreal branch of the Royal Bank of Canada, which were cashed in London.

country_links.evidence_quote: During the 1960s, the Kray twins formed an alliance with the Commission, the governing body of the American Mafia. The brothers were in contact with Meyer Lansky and Angelo Bruno, two U.S. gangsters who were looking to invest in London's nightclubs and casinos to engage in money laundering.
country_links.context: The Firm formed an alliance with the American Mafia, including Meyer Lansky and Angelo Bruno, who were looking to invest in London's nightclubs and casinos.
